value: {"cycleId":"f7cbb1bb-1f43-4f29-9103-1718f0c7c653","sprintVersion":"9.0.0","verdict":"APPROVE","rationale":"Sprint v9.0.0 delivers the core memory wiring objective end-to-end (schema, write paths in cycle/gate/review phases, read paths in audit/execute, dashboard surface, and integration test) with 18/20 items completed and strong test coverage. Code reviewer issued APPROVE with only one MAJOR (hardcoded import.meta.url paths in flywheel.ts вҖ” latent, not live) and minor structural nits. The two failed items (/approvals wiring and /branches tab) are isolated dashboard routes that don't block the memory layer shipping and can be re-queued for v9.1 alongside the reviewer's cleanup suggestions. Cost ($15.61) is well within envelope.","criticalFindings":["\"response\": \"`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n**The memory type schism** fixed in `execute-phase.ts` is a classic API evolution hazard вҖ” when writers use `id` (canonical UUID) and readers expect `key` (human-readable slug), the labels silently render as `undefined` in prompts. The broadened local interface (`id?: string; key?: string`) is the right fix: both shapes parse cleanly and `key ?? id ?? type` always resolves a non-empty label. Re-exporting a stricter external type (`CycleMemoryEntry` with `id: string` required) would have made test fixtures that only supply `key` fail at the type-checker вҖ” keeping the type local-and-loose avoids that brittleness.\\n`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review: AgentForge Sprint v9.0.0 / commit `feat(v6.7.4)`\\n\\n### What This Change Is\\n\\nA \\\"salvage\\\" commit that cherry-picks the cross-cycle memory layer from a gate-rejected cycle (`ee9580bd`) plus two focused bug fixes (MemoryEntry type schism, `approvalsRoutes` signature), adds a new `/api/v5/flywheel` route, refactors the approvals dashboard to cycle-based UX, and adds ~330 lines of new tests.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe change does exactly what the commit message claims. The three thread counts line up:\\n\\n| Claimed | Delivered |\\n|---|---|\\n| Fix `type` key duplication in `logGitEvent` / `logPREvent` | вң… Verified in diff |\\n| Revert `approvalsRoutes(app, opts)` вҶ’ `approvalsRoutes(app)` | вң… Confirmed |\\n| Memory wiring: `cycle-logger`, `audit-phase`, `gate-phase` | вң… Salvaged cleanly |\\n| New `/api/v5/flywheel` endpoint with 4 gauges + `memoryStats` | вң… Route registered, tested |\\n| Review phase writes `review-finding` memory entries per CRITICAL/MAJOR | вң… Implemented and tested |\\n| Markdown code-fence guard in `proposal-to-backlog` | вң… Implemented and tested (line 261 in test file) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**MAJOR вҖ” Hardcoded absolute paths in `flywheel.ts` (not injection-safe, but fragile)**\\n```ts\\n// src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18\\nconst MEMORY_JSONL_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/memory');\\nconst CYCLES_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/cycles');\\n```\\n`computeMemoryStats()` and `computeVelocity()` compute `projectRoot` from `import.meta.url`, while every other route receives it via options (e.g., `opts.adapter`, `opts.projectRoot`). If the build output tree changes or the server is started from a symlinked path, these silently resolve to the wrong directory. The functions should accept `projectRoot: string` as a parameter (or read it from opts), consistent with the pattern in `execute-phase.ts` and `review-phase.ts`.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” Silent error swallowing in both flywheel route catch blocks**\\n```ts\\n// Lines 443, 484\\n} catch {\\n  // returns zero-state вҖ” original error is silently dropped, not even console.warn'd\\n}\\n```\\nThe `v1` and `v5` catch blocks return valid zero-state objects, which is correct for UI resilience, but they log nothing. A `console.warn` or a Fastify `log.warn` call would help operators diagnose issues in production. Compare with the rest of the codebase which uses `console.warn` in similar fallback cases.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” `review-phase.ts` depends on `gate-phase.ts` via `extractFindingsByLevel`**\\n```ts\\nimport { extractFindingsByLevel } from './gate-phase.js';\\n```\\nReview runs *before* gate in the 9-phase pipeline, so this reverse import is an architectural smell. `extractFindingsByLevel` is a pure text-parsing utility and would be better placed in a shared `phase-utils.ts` or colocated in `review-phase.ts` itself. Not a bug, but it muddies the layering.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” Global approval store uses `var` throughout the IIFE**\\nThe new `initApprovalStore` IIFE in `dashboard/index.html` uses `var` declarations exclusively, which is inconsistent with the `const`/`let` used everywhere else in the same file. Pre-ES5 style in 2026 is fine for a legacy HTML file but signals the agent wasn't style-matching.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” `computeVelocity`/`computeInheritance` vs. `computeMetaLearning`/`computeAutonomy` вҖ” inconsistent signatures**\\nTwo helpers accept `adapter: SqliteAdapter` and two hit the filesystem independently. This makes unit-testing the filesystem-backed helpers harder (no injection point for a mock `projectRoot`). A minor structural inconsistency now but it will compound.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo SQL injection, XSS, or credential exposure introduced. The approval modal uses `textContent` for all user-supplied values, and cycle IDs are `encodeURIComponent`-escaped before being included in fetch URLs. вң…\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nCoverage for new functionality is solid:\\n- `flywheel-v5.test.ts` вҖ” 20+ contract + data-driven tests, including zero-state, cap validation, and score math.\\n- `phase-handlers-verification.test.ts` вҖ” 5 memory-persistence tests for review phase, including \\\"no entry when only MINOR findings.\\\"\\n- `proposal-to-backlog.test.ts` вҖ” Code-fence guard has a proper regression test at line 261.\\n- `sprint-framework.test.ts` вҖ” 15 lifecycle + gate-verdict memory integration tests.\\n\\n**Gap:** No test covers the case where `computeVelocity()` reads actual sprint files. The filesystem helpers are tested only via the HTTP contract (zero-state assertions), meaning a regression in the file-reading logic would not be caught until the endpoint returned wrong numbers.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE\\nSUMMARY: Solid salvage commit вҖ” memory wiring, flywheel gauges, and approvals UX all correct and well-tested; two minor structural issues (hardcoded paths, reverse import) should be addressed in a follow-up item\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MAJOR]  computeMemoryStats/computeVelocity use import.meta.url-relative paths instead of injected projectRoot вҖ” fragile if build layout changes (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18, 296)\\n  - [MINOR]  Both flywheel catch blocks swallow errors without logging (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:443, 484)\\n  - [MINOR]  review-phase.ts imports extractFindingsByLevel from gate-phase.ts вҖ” reverse pipeline dependency (packages/core/src/autonomous/phase-handlers/review-phase.ts:8)\\n  - [MINOR]  Global approval store IIFE uses var throughout; inconsistent with rest of file (dashboard/index.html)\\n  - [MINOR]  computeVelocity/computeInheritance don't accept projectRoot parameter, making them untestable in isolation\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Move extractFindingsByLevel to a shared phase-utils.ts to break the reviewвҶ’gate import\\n  - Add a filesystem-seeded unit test for computeVelocity() to guard the sprint-file parsing logic\\n  - Add console.warn in the flywheel catch blocks for operator visibility\\n```\\n\\n**Overall: 4 / 5** вҖ” Ship it. The MAJOR path issue is a latent risk but not a live bug (the current build layout works), and the test coverage delta is strongly positive. The structural issues are good candidates for a v9.1 cleanup ticket.\""],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n**The memory type schism** fixed in `execute-phase.ts` is a classic API evolution hazard вҖ” when writers use `id` (canonical UUID) and readers expect `key` (human-readable slug), the labels silently render as `undefined` in prompts. The broadened local interface (`id?: string; key?: string`) is the right fix: both shapes parse cleanly and `key ?? id ?? type` always resolves a non-empty label. Re-exporting a stricter external type (`CycleMemoryEntry` with `id: string` required) would have made test fixtures that only supply `key` fail at the type-checker вҖ” keeping the type local-and-loose avoids that brittleness.\\n`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review: AgentForge Sprint v9.0.0 / commit `feat(v6.7.4)`\\n\\n### What This Change Is\\n\\nA \\\"salvage\\\" commit that cherry-picks the cross-cycle memory layer from a gate-rejected cycle (`ee9580bd`) plus two focused bug fixes (MemoryEntry type schism, `approvalsRoutes` signature), adds a new `/api/v5/flywheel` route, refactors the approvals dashboard to cycle-based UX, and adds ~330 lines of new tests.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe change does exactly what the commit message claims. The three thread counts line up:\\n\\n| Claimed | Delivered |\\n|---|---|\\n| Fix `type` key duplication in `logGitEvent` / `logPREvent` | вң… Verified in diff |\\n| Revert `approvalsRoutes(app, opts)` вҶ’ `approvalsRoutes(app)` | вң… Confirmed |\\n| Memory wiring: `cycle-logger`, `audit-phase`, `gate-phase` | вң… Salvaged cleanly |\\n| New `/api/v5/flywheel` endpoint with 4 gauges + `memoryStats` | вң… Route registered, tested |\\n| Review phase writes `review-finding` memory entries per CRITICAL/MAJOR | вң… Implemented and tested |\\n| Markdown code-fence guard in `proposal-to-backlog` | вң… Implemented and tested (line 261 in test file) |\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**MAJOR вҖ” Hardcoded absolute paths in `flywheel.ts` (not injection-safe, but fragile)**\\n```ts\\n// src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18\\nconst MEMORY_JSONL_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/memory');\\nconst CYCLES_DIR = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../.agentforge/cycles');\\n```\\n`computeMemoryStats()` and `computeVelocity()` compute `projectRoot` from `import.meta.url`, while every other route receives it via options (e.g., `opts.adapter`, `opts.projectRoot`). If the build output tree changes or the server is started from a symlinked path, these silently resolve to the wrong directory. The functions should accept `projectRoot: string` as a parameter (or read it from opts), consistent with the pattern in `execute-phase.ts` and `review-phase.ts`.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” Silent error swallowing in both flywheel route catch blocks**\\n```ts\\n// Lines 443, 484\\n} catch {\\n  // returns zero-state вҖ” original error is silently dropped, not even console.warn'd\\n}\\n```\\nThe `v1` and `v5` catch blocks return valid zero-state objects, which is correct for UI resilience, but they log nothing. A `console.warn` or a Fastify `log.warn` call would help operators diagnose issues in production. Compare with the rest of the codebase which uses `console.warn` in similar fallback cases.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” `review-phase.ts` depends on `gate-phase.ts` via `extractFindingsByLevel`**\\n```ts\\nimport { extractFindingsByLevel } from './gate-phase.js';\\n```\\nReview runs *before* gate in the 9-phase pipeline, so this reverse import is an architectural smell. `extractFindingsByLevel` is a pure text-parsing utility and would be better placed in a shared `phase-utils.ts` or colocated in `review-phase.ts` itself. Not a bug, but it muddies the layering.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” Global approval store uses `var` throughout the IIFE**\\nThe new `initApprovalStore` IIFE in `dashboard/index.html` uses `var` declarations exclusively, which is inconsistent with the `const`/`let` used everywhere else in the same file. Pre-ES5 style in 2026 is fine for a legacy HTML file but signals the agent wasn't style-matching.\\n\\n**MINOR вҖ” `computeVelocity`/`computeInheritance` vs. `computeMetaLearning`/`computeAutonomy` вҖ” inconsistent signatures**\\nTwo helpers accept `adapter: SqliteAdapter` and two hit the filesystem independently. This makes unit-testing the filesystem-backed helpers harder (no injection point for a mock `projectRoot`). A minor structural inconsistency now but it will compound.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo SQL injection, XSS, or credential exposure introduced. The approval modal uses `textContent` for all user-supplied values, and cycle IDs are `encodeURIComponent`-escaped before being included in fetch URLs. вң…\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nCoverage for new functionality is solid:\\n- `flywheel-v5.test.ts` вҖ” 20+ contract + data-driven tests, including zero-state, cap validation, and score math.\\n- `phase-handlers-verification.test.ts` вҖ” 5 memory-persistence tests for review phase, including \\\"no entry when only MINOR findings.\\\"\\n- `proposal-to-backlog.test.ts` вҖ” Code-fence guard has a proper regression test at line 261.\\n- `sprint-framework.test.ts` вҖ” 15 lifecycle + gate-verdict memory integration tests.\\n\\n**Gap:** No test covers the case where `computeVelocity()` reads actual sprint files. The filesystem helpers are tested only via the HTTP contract (zero-state assertions), meaning a regression in the file-reading logic would not be caught until the endpoint returned wrong numbers.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: APPROVE\\nSUMMARY: Solid salvage commit вҖ” memory wiring, flywheel gauges, and approvals UX all correct and well-tested; two minor structural issues (hardcoded paths, reverse import) should be addressed in a follow-up item\\nISSUES:\\n  - [MAJOR]  computeMemoryStats/computeVelocity use import.meta.url-relative paths instead of injected projectRoot вҖ” fragile if build layout changes (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:8-18, 296)\\n  - [MINOR]  Both flywheel catch blocks swallow errors without logging (src/server/routes/flywheel.ts:443, 484)\\n  - [MINOR]  review-phase.ts imports extractFindingsByLevel from gate-phase.ts вҖ” reverse pipeline dependency (packages/core/src/autonomous/phase-handlers/review-phase.ts:8)\\n  - [MINOR]  Global approval store IIFE uses var throughout; inconsistent with rest of file (dashboard/index.html)\\n  - [MINOR]  computeVelocity/computeInheritance don't accept projectRoot parameter, making them untestable in isolation\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Move extractFindingsByLevel to a shared phase-utils.ts to break the reviewвҶ’gate import\\n  - Add a filesystem-seeded unit test for computeVelocity() to guard the sprint-file parsing logic\\n  - Add console.warn in the flywheel catch blocks for operator visibility\\n```\\n\\n**Overall: 4 / 5** вҖ” Ship it. The MAJOR path issue is a latent risk but not a live bug (the current build layout works), and the test coverage delta is strongly positive. The structural issues are good candidates for a v9.1 cleanup ticket.\""]}

{"cycleId":"bbcde323-e272-4da4-bf44-1b2836ae4cbc","sprintVersion":"9.0.1","verdict":"REJECT","rationale":"The sprint delivered strong feature breadth вҖ” 19 of 20 items completed, solid test additions, and good architectural patterns вҖ” but the CRITICAL gate-verdict bug is a non-negotiable blocker. The gate phase is the core self-correction mechanism of the autonomous loop: if REJECT verdicts are silently mis-tagged as APPROVE, the system loses its ability to learn from failures, which is the entire point of the memory-wiring sprint. Shipping this would embed a silent correctness regression into the learning loop that compounds over every subsequent cycle. The two meta.total pagination bugs further erode dashboard trust. Fix the three real bugs (CRITICAL + 2 MAJOR), re-run the gate-phase test to confirm REJECT propagation, and resubmit вҖ” the underlying work is sound and should merge cleanly once these are addressed.","criticalFindings":["\"response\": \"`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\nThe uncommitted changes show 28 files with 1,767 net new lines. Running tests against both states reveals the sprint **reduced total failures from 44 вҶ’ 31** (13 pre-existing tests fixed), but introduced 1 new regression and carries two `meta.total` correctness bugs across the new filtered memory endpoints.\\n`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review вҖ” Sprint v9.0.1\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe sprint's stated goals (gate-verdict memory, memory endpoint filters, cross-cycle audit propagation, SSE v5 stream, v5 branch endpoints, DRY branch-route refactor) are all present and structurally sound. The execute-phase cap-D fix (`Math.min(itemCount, ceilingParallelism)`) is correct and the test was updated to match the new optimistic parallelism behavior. Most of the work is solid.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**[CRITICAL] REJECT verdict mis-tagged as APPROVE вҖ” `packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:661`**\\n\\nThe new `runGatePhase` reads verdict from the sprint file _after_ `runLlmPhase` completes. Since `runLlmPhase` appends a new `phaseResult` entry, `.pop()` returns the LLM phase's freshly-written result вҖ” not any seeded historical entry. In the test fixture this overwritten entry contains the mock agent's response (typically empty/approve-shaped), so a sprint seeded with `\\\"REJECT: test coverage too low\\\"` is silently tagged `verdict:approve`. This is confirmed by `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` failing with `expected ['verdict:approve', вҖҰ] to include 'verdict:reject'`. The response should be read from the `RunResult` returned directly by `runLlmPhase`, not scraped from disk.\\n\\n**[MAJOR] `meta.total` reports paginated count, not total matches вҖ” `packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts` and `src/server/routes/memory.ts`**\\n\\n```ts\\n// dashboard-stubs.ts\\nconst data = filtered.slice(0, pageLimit);\\nreturn reply.send({ data, meta: { total: data.length } }); // вҶҗ data.length is sliced\\n```\\n\\n`meta.total` is supposed to tell the client how many entries _match_ the filters before pagination. After `.slice(0, pageLimit)`, `data.length` вүӨ `pageLimit`, not the true match count. Any UI that renders \\\"showing X of Y\\\" or drives infinite scroll will misbehave. Should be `filtered.length`.\\n\\n**[MINOR] `readGateVerdict()` is tightly coupled to internal file layout вҖ” `packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134`**\\n\\n`CycleLogger` now reaches directly into `cycles/<id>/phases/gate.json` to read structured verdict data. This dual-write path (phase handler writes gate.json вҶ’ cycle logger re-reads it) creates an implicit ordering contract. If the gate phase ever writes under a different path or format, the method silently returns `null` with no warning. Passing the verdict explicitly through `logCycleResult`'s parameter struct would be safer.\\n\\n**[MINOR] Version hardcode stale in integration test вҖ” `tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22`**\\n\\n```ts\\nexpect(body.version).toBe('6.1.0');\\n```\\n\\nPackage version is now `6.8.0`. Pre-existing failure, but the sprint changed `server.ts` and this test is in scope вҖ” it should have been updated.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo injection vectors, no credential exposure. The branch-delete endpoint already enforces `autonomous/*` prefix via `deleteBranchByName`. Memory writes are fire-and-forget inside `catch {}` вҖ” appropriate.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nNew test files are strong: `memory.test.ts` covers all four new query params with edge cases, `branches.test.ts` and `sprints.test.ts` are comprehensive, and the two-cycle `memory-flow.test.ts` suite is the right integration anchor for the learning loop. The `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` suite is well-designed вҖ” it caught the real REJECT bug it was written to guard. The `sprint-framework.test.ts` additions correctly validate the new `recordReviewFindings` delegation.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: REQUEST_CHANGES\\nSUMMARY: Strong feature set; one real correctness bug (REJECT mis-tagged APPROVE) and \\n         two meta.total reporting bugs need fixing before merge.\\nISSUES:\\n  - [CRITICAL] Gate verdict reads wrong phaseResult after runLlmPhase appends a new \\n    entry; REJECT sprint is tagged verdict:approve вҖ” packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:655-662\\n  - [MAJOR] meta.total reflects post-slice count, not total filter matches вҖ” \\n    packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts:168 and src/server/routes/memory.ts:330\\n  - [MINOR] readGateVerdict() implicit file-layout coupling in CycleLogger вҖ” \\n    packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134\\n  - [MINOR] Stale version assertion in integration test (6.1.0 vs 6.8.0) вҖ” \\n    tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Pass verdict as a field on RunResult (or a wrapper) rather than re-reading disk\\n  - Consider adding a total-before-cap field (e.g., meta: { total: data.length, \\n    totalMatching: filtered.length }) so clients can distinguish cap vs filter count\\n  - The SSE handler extraction (handleSseConnection) is clean вҖ” good pattern to continue\\n  - listBranches() / deleteBranchByName() DRY refactor is textbook correct\\n```\\n\\n**Overall score: 3 / 5** вҖ” Good architectural progress and meaningful test additions, blocked by the REJECT-verdict bug which is a correctness issue in the core learning loop.\""],"majorFindings":["\"response\": \"`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\nThe uncommitted changes show 28 files with 1,767 net new lines. Running tests against both states reveals the sprint **reduced total failures from 44 вҶ’ 31** (13 pre-existing tests fixed), but introduced 1 new regression and carries two `meta.total` correctness bugs across the new filtered memory endpoints.\\n`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Code Review вҖ” Sprint v9.0.1\\n\\n### Overall Correctness\\n\\nThe sprint's stated goals (gate-verdict memory, memory endpoint filters, cross-cycle audit propagation, SSE v5 stream, v5 branch endpoints, DRY branch-route refactor) are all present and structurally sound. The execute-phase cap-D fix (`Math.min(itemCount, ceilingParallelism)`) is correct and the test was updated to match the new optimistic parallelism behavior. Most of the work is solid.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Issues\\n\\n**[CRITICAL] REJECT verdict mis-tagged as APPROVE вҖ” `packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:661`**\\n\\nThe new `runGatePhase` reads verdict from the sprint file _after_ `runLlmPhase` completes. Since `runLlmPhase` appends a new `phaseResult` entry, `.pop()` returns the LLM phase's freshly-written result вҖ” not any seeded historical entry. In the test fixture this overwritten entry contains the mock agent's response (typically empty/approve-shaped), so a sprint seeded with `\\\"REJECT: test coverage too low\\\"` is silently tagged `verdict:approve`. This is confirmed by `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` failing with `expected ['verdict:approve', вҖҰ] to include 'verdict:reject'`. The response should be read from the `RunResult` returned directly by `runLlmPhase`, not scraped from disk.\\n\\n**[MAJOR] `meta.total` reports paginated count, not total matches вҖ” `packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts` and `src/server/routes/memory.ts`**\\n\\n```ts\\n// dashboard-stubs.ts\\nconst data = filtered.slice(0, pageLimit);\\nreturn reply.send({ data, meta: { total: data.length } }); // вҶҗ data.length is sliced\\n```\\n\\n`meta.total` is supposed to tell the client how many entries _match_ the filters before pagination. After `.slice(0, pageLimit)`, `data.length` вүӨ `pageLimit`, not the true match count. Any UI that renders \\\"showing X of Y\\\" or drives infinite scroll will misbehave. Should be `filtered.length`.\\n\\n**[MINOR] `readGateVerdict()` is tightly coupled to internal file layout вҖ” `packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134`**\\n\\n`CycleLogger` now reaches directly into `cycles/<id>/phases/gate.json` to read structured verdict data. This dual-write path (phase handler writes gate.json вҶ’ cycle logger re-reads it) creates an implicit ordering contract. If the gate phase ever writes under a different path or format, the method silently returns `null` with no warning. Passing the verdict explicitly through `logCycleResult`'s parameter struct would be safer.\\n\\n**[MINOR] Version hardcode stale in integration test вҖ” `tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22`**\\n\\n```ts\\nexpect(body.version).toBe('6.1.0');\\n```\\n\\nPackage version is now `6.8.0`. Pre-existing failure, but the sprint changed `server.ts` and this test is in scope вҖ” it should have been updated.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Security\\n\\nNo injection vectors, no credential exposure. The branch-delete endpoint already enforces `autonomous/*` prefix via `deleteBranchByName`. Memory writes are fire-and-forget inside `catch {}` вҖ” appropriate.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Test Coverage\\n\\nNew test files are strong: `memory.test.ts` covers all four new query params with edge cases, `branches.test.ts` and `sprints.test.ts` are comprehensive, and the two-cycle `memory-flow.test.ts` suite is the right integration anchor for the learning loop. The `gate-phase-memory.test.ts` suite is well-designed вҖ” it caught the real REJECT bug it was written to guard. The `sprint-framework.test.ts` additions correctly validate the new `recordReviewFindings` delegation.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Verdict\\n\\n```\\nVERDICT: REQUEST_CHANGES\\nSUMMARY: Strong feature set; one real correctness bug (REJECT mis-tagged APPROVE) and \\n         two meta.total reporting bugs need fixing before merge.\\nISSUES:\\n  - [CRITICAL] Gate verdict reads wrong phaseResult after runLlmPhase appends a new \\n    entry; REJECT sprint is tagged verdict:approve вҖ” packages/server/src/lib/phase-handlers.ts:655-662\\n  - [MAJOR] meta.total reflects post-slice count, not total filter matches вҖ” \\n    packages/server/src/routes/v5/dashboard-stubs.ts:168 and src/server/routes/memory.ts:330\\n  - [MINOR] readGateVerdict() implicit file-layout coupling in CycleLogger вҖ” \\n    packages/core/src/autonomous/cycle-logger.ts:134\\n  - [MINOR] Stale version assertion in integration test (6.1.0 vs 6.8.0) вҖ” \\n    tests/v5/server-integration.test.ts:22\\nSUGGESTIONS:\\n  - Pass verdict as a field on RunResult (or a wrapper) rather than re-reading disk\\n  - Consider adding a total-before-cap field (e.g., meta: { total: data.length, \\n    totalMatching: filtered.length }) so clients can distinguish cap vs filter count\\n  - The SSE handler extraction (handleSseConnection) is clean вҖ” good pattern to continue\\n  - listBranches() / deleteBranchByName() DRY refactor is textbook correct\\n```\\n\\n**Overall score: 3 / 5** вҖ” Good architectural progress and meaningful test additions, blocked by the REJECT-verdict bug which is a correctness issue in the core learning loop.\""]}
